name: Philippines
description: 7,641 islands — Manila, Magellan, Marcos, and the country of jeepneys and karaoke.

questions.q: What is the capital of the Philippines?

source.notes: Quezon City is the most populous city; Manila is the capital.

questions.q: How many islands make up the Philippines?

source.notes: Officially 7,641 islands.

questions.q: What is the official language of the Philippines (along with English)?

source.notes: Filipino is standardized form of Tagalog. English is co-official.

questions.q: Which European explorer arrived in the Philippines in 1521 and was killed there?

source.notes: Killed by Lapu-Lapu's warriors at the Battle of Mactan, April 27, 1521.

questions.q: Who is the Filipino chieftain who killed Magellan and is celebrated as a national hero?

source.notes: Battle of Mactan, 1521.

questions.q: Approximately how many years was the Philippines under Spanish rule?

source.notes: From Legazpi's arrival in 1565 to U.S. takeover in 1898.

questions.q: Who was the first president of the Philippines?

source.notes: Declared independence on June 12, 1898.

questions.q: Which Filipino national hero authored 'Noli Me Tangere' and 'El Filibusterismo' before being executed in 1896?

source.notes: Executed by firing squad on December 30, 1896 at Bagumbayan (now Rizal Park).

questions.q: When did the Philippines gain full independence from the United States?

source.notes: July 4, 1946 (now June 12 — Aguinaldo's 1898 declaration).

questions.q: Which Filipino dictator ruled 1965-1986, declared martial law, and was ousted by the People Power Revolution?

source.notes: EDSA Revolution February 1986.

questions.q: Whose son became Philippine president in 2022 — son of the Marcos dictator?

source.notes: Won June 2022 election.

questions.q: Which 1986 'People Power Revolution' overthrew Marcos and put Cory Aquino in office?

source.notes: Both names work — yellow was the rallying color, EDSA was the highway.

questions.q: Which famous Filipino delicacy is a partially-formed duck embryo egg?

source.notes: Eaten as street food, traditionally with salt + vinegar.

questions.q: What is the most popular Filipino dish — meat braised in vinegar, soy sauce, garlic?

source.notes: Often called the 'unofficial' national dish. Chicken or pork.

questions.q: What is the cool, mountain-resort city known as the 'Summer Capital of the Philippines'?

source.notes: Cooler high-elevation city north of Manila.

questions.q: Which Filipino island is famous for its 4-km White Beach and surf?

source.notes: Repeatedly voted 'world's best beach.'

questions.q: Which Philippine island is the 'Surfing Capital of the Philippines'?

source.notes: Famous for the 'Cloud 9' wave.

questions.q: Which 1991 Philippine volcanic eruption was the second-largest of the 20th century?

source.notes: June 15, 1991. Cooled global temperatures by ~0.5°C for 2 years.

questions.q: Which Filipino boxer became an 8-division world champion?

source.notes: Pacquiao also served as a Senator.

questions.q: Which traditional Filipino vehicle is a colorful, decorated mini-bus made from reused jeeps?

source.notes: Originally made from WWII US military jeeps.
